pricingLayerBreakdown.reasoning:
Multi-line quote: 2 line(s).
[kyry44cj] carpentry @ £320.00 ref → £352.00 LLM → £352.00 guarded. Carpentry (loft hatch + ladder installation). Reference £320 (32000p) for 480min at £40/hr. We're supplying materials (loft hatch + ladder kit), so add materials markup (~10% on labour = 3200p). Realistic working time is 480min (8hrs) — this is a full-day job involving access, frame prep, hatch fitting, and ladder assembly. No urgency or time-of-service premiums apply. New customer, so no loyalty discount. Total: (480min ÷ 60 × £40/hr × 100p) + materials markup = 32000p + 3200p = 35200p.
[ct7l2etb] door_fitting @ £140.00 ref → £135.00 LLM → £140.00 guarded. Door fitting (4-panel internal door with frame). Category: PER-UNIT. Reference £140 (14000p) per door. Count: 1 door unit. Base calculation: 1 unit × £140 = 14000p. However, we are supplying the door and frame, so apply materials markup (~10% on labour component ≈ 1400p, but reference already includes typical supply; net adjustment is modest). Realistic time: 240min (4hrs) for frame prep, hanging, and adjustment. No urgency or time-of-service premiums. New customer, no loyalty discount. Suggested price: 13500p (slightly below reference to reflect competitive positioning for new customer acquisition, but within acceptable range given materials supply and standard complexity).
Labour subtotal: £492.00.
Materials (with 27% margin): £439.00.
Batch discount (labour only): 8% = -£39.00.
Guardrails: [ct7l2etb] Floor: £135.00 raised to £140.00 (£35.00/hr x 4.00hr) | Batch discount: 8% off subtotal £492.00 = -£39.00
Final: £892.00.

batchDiscount.reasoning: Two jobs in one visit (loft hatch + door fitting) saves travel and setup time. Both are carpentry/fitting tasks that can be sequenced efficiently in a single day. 8% discount reflects moderate efficiency gain.
